summary: Enlarged blind spots can be caused by raised intraocular pressure related to conditions affecting cranio-orbital space dynamics, notably seen in cases of papilledema rather than diseases primarily impacting peripheral retinal structures alone. Amongst available choices, the association aligns more closely with a scenario involving altered intracranial pressure indicative of papilledema over alternatives focusing mainly on periphery retina integrity issues without similar spatial impact patterns observable clinically here presented specifically addressing this query about enlargements noted within physiological limits defined
